reforms.description: The government plans to provide SMEs in manufacturing and services with innovation vouchers that can be used to commission R&D and studies on potential for new technology introduction, aiming to boost innovation and productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to R&D, SMEs can enhance their innovation capabilities, leading to improved productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Subsidies to SMEs should focus more on promoting growth and boosting innovation and productivity.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of innovation vouchers represents a targeted effort to enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, which is crucial for overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Provide SMEs in manufacturing and services with innovation vouchers that can be used to commission R&D and studies on potential for new technology introduction.

reforms.description: The government announced a financial support package of KRW 10.4 trillion for export companies, start-ups, and ventures, which includes loans and guarantees specifically aimed at these sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This support is expected to enhance the growth of innovative firms, thereby contributing to economic recovery and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The financial support package was announced and is being executed.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant financial intervention aimed at bolstering the start-up ecosystem during a crisis, which is crucial for innovation and economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: On 8 April, the government announced a 10.4 trillion financial support package for export companies, start-ups and ventures.

reforms.description: The government expanded support for lowering R&D costs by KRW 2.2 trillion as part of the financial support package for start-ups and ventures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering R&D costs encourages innovation and helps firms to maintain their research activities during economic downturns.
reforms.status_evidence: The government included this support in the financial package for start-ups and ventures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms aimed at fostering R&D in the private sector during a challenging economic period.
reforms.source_quote: The package includes expanded government support for lowering R&D costs (KRW 2.2 trillion).

reforms.description: The government promotes numerous projects relying on 5G technology to increase competitiveness and innovation in industries such as smart manufacturing, smart grids, and smart healthcare, aiming to enhance the quality of life and solve social problems.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The promotion of 5G technology is expected to lead to higher production, exports, and employment, thereby boosting overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government promotes numerous projects relying on 5G to increase competitiveness and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the approach to leveraging digital technology for economic growth, with significant implications for various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s plan for innovative growth focusses on digital opportunities raised by the use of 5G.

reforms.description: The promotion of innovation networks between SMEs, academia, and large firms through vouchers or platforms is suggested to support SMEs' R&D and commercialization of innovative goods and services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration between SMEs and larger entities is expected to enhance R&D capabilities and lead to the commercialization of innovative products, thus driving economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Promoting innovation networks between SMEs, academia and large firms through vouchers or platforms can support SMEs’ R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, which are crucial for economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Promoting innovation networks between SMEs, academia and large firms through vouchers or platforms can support SMEs’ R&D and commercialization of innovative goods and services.

reforms.description: The Korean government has introduced a programme to shift the burden of proof from the regulated to the regulator, allowing firms in new technologies to test their products and business models without being subject to all existing legal requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform encourages innovation by reducing regulatory barriers, enabling firms to experiment and develop new technologies, which can enhance productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has introduced a programme to facilitate testing of new technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in regulatory approach, promoting innovation and experimentation in emerging sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced a programme to shift the burden of proof from the regulated to the regulator and regulatory sandboxes are allowing firms in new technologies and new industries to test their products and business models without being subject to all existing legal requirements.

reforms.description: The government plans to reallocate financial support for technology R&D to focus on commercialization for SMEs that successfully developed new technology, addressing the gap in funding for product launches.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the commercialization of new technologies, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Reallocate financial support for technology R&D to commercialization for SMEs that successfully developed new technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical need for funding in the commercialization phase, which is essential for translating R&D into marketable products.
reforms.source_quote: Reallocate financial support for technology R&D to commercialization for SMEs that successfully developed new technology.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced the Korean New Deal, which aims to boost investments in digital, green, and skills development to support a green and inclusive recovery from the COVID-19 crisis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By investing in digital and green technologies, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and create jobs, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set ambitious targets for the share of renewables and digital investments as part of the Korean New Deal.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Korean New Deal represents a significant shift in policy focus towards sustainable and inclusive growth, with potential long-term impacts on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The recently announced Korean New Deal, which will boost digital, green and skills investments, has the potential to support a green and inclusive recovery.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced a New Deal to revive the economy by enhancing digitalisation and facilitating the convergence of new and old industries, focusing on projects that strengthen data infrastructures and develop artificial intelligence.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The New Deal aims to boost productivity and innovation through enhanced digitalisation and infrastructure development, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Korean authorities recently announced a Korean New Deal to revive the economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The New Deal represents a significant structural change aimed at modernising the economy through digitalisation and innovation, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Korean authorities recently announced a Korean New deal to revive the economy, by facilitating the convergence of new and old industries through enhanced use of digitalisation.

reforms.description: The Korean government supports R&D through the Korea Small Business Innovation Research (KOSBIR) programme and R&D grants to SMEs, which have contributed to lift corporate R&D investment and registration of intellectual property rights. However, the effectiveness of these support programmes is under review, and innovation vouchers are proposed to better direct R&D subsidies towards innovative SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging SMEs to engage in innovative projects can enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Support programmes should be reviewed and innovation vouchers should be introduced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of innovation vouchers represents a significant shift in how R&D subsidies are directed, aiming to improve the effectiveness of support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Support programmes should be reviewed and innovation vouchers should be introduced to better direct R&D subsidies towards innovative SMEs.

reforms.description: The government has launched regulatory sandboxes to allow firms in new technologies and new industries to test their products and business models without being subject to all existing legal requirements, facilitating innovation and reducing regulatory burdens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers, firms can innovate more freely, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched regulatory sandboxes in ICT convergence, industrial convergence, financial innovation and regional innovation since January 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of regulatory sandboxes represents a significant reform in regulatory policy, aimed at fostering innovation in emerging sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched regulatory sandboxes in ICT convergence, industrial convergence, financial innovation and regional innovation since January 2019.

reforms.description: The Ministry of SMEs and Startups has introduced a budget plan for 2020 amounting to KRW 13.5 trillion to promote manufacturing innovation in SMEs and support their scaling-up, including measures for smart factories and R&D collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The budget aims to enhance productivity and innovation in SMEs, which can lead to increased economic growth and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget plan was put into effect to support various innovation measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the innovation ecosystem for SMEs, aiming to transform the manufacturing sector and enhance competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of SMEs and Startups (MSS) 2020 budget plan amounts to KRW 13.5 trillion (about USD 11.5 billion), up from KRW 10.3 trillion for 2019 and mainly includes measures to promote manufacturing innovation in SMEs and support their scaling-up.

reforms.description: The government plans to support 300 start-ups in key sectors such as semiconductors and bio-health, along with increasing the budget for the Tech Incubator Programme for Start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering start-ups in high-potential sectors, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funds to support start-ups in various sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a targeted effort to enhance the start-up ecosystem, which is crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Support 300 start-ups in the fields of semiconductors, bio-health and future cars (KRW 45 billion); set up a mentoring project between retired experts and start-ups (KRW 6 billion).

reforms.description: The Korean government initiated a strategy in December 2019 to promote the adoption of artificial intelligence, which includes making AI courses compulsory in primary and secondary schools and opening AI departments in colleges.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to build a skilled workforce in AI, which is essential for enhancing productivity and innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has started to take steps in this direction by promoting plans to train more experts in fourth industrial revolution core technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in educational policy aimed at equipping future generations with essential skills for a rapidly evolving digital landscape.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2019, the government initiated a strategy promoting the adoption of artificial intelligence, which includes making artificial intelligence courses compulsory in primary and secondary schools.

reforms.description: The Korean government supports SMEs through the Korea Small Business Innovation Research (KOSBIR) programme, which focuses on R&D grants to enhance innovation and productivity among small enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted R&D funding, the KOSBIR programme aims to enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, leading to increased productivity and economic benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: The KOSBIR programme has been in operation since 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The KOSBIR programme represents a significant effort to support SMEs through R&D funding, although it has been in place for several years and is not a new initiative.
reforms.source_quote: The Korean government supports SMEs through the Korea Small Business Innovation Research (KOSBIR) programme and R&D grants since 1998.

reforms.description: The introduction of innovation vouchers for SMEs aims to encourage engagement in innovative projects and facilitate collaboration with research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Innovation vouchers are designed to stimulate R&D activities among SMEs, leading to increased innovation and competitiveness in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The concept of innovation vouchers has been discussed as a means to support SMEs in Korea.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of innovation vouchers represents a significant policy tool aimed at enhancing SME innovation, although it is not yet implemented.
reforms.source_quote: Providing SMEs in manufacturing and services with innovation vouchers would encourage them to engage in innovative projects.

reforms.description: The Korean government introduced regulatory sandboxes in January 2019 to allow firms to experiment with innovative products and business models without being subject to all existing legal requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers, the initiative encourages innovation and investment in new technologies, enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has approved numerous projects under the regulatory sandbox system since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of regulatory sandboxes represents a significant shift in how regulations interact with innovation, fostering a more dynamic environment for startups and new technologies.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced regulatory sandboxes since January 2019 in areas such as FinTech, the medical sector, manufacturing, electronics, telecommunication, energy and mobility.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced the designation of regulation-free special zones to allow firms to experiment with innovative technologies in designated areas without regulatory restrictions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These zones promote innovation by enabling firms to test new technologies and business models, which can lead to increased investment and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the designation of regulation-free special zones.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the initiative is part of a broader strategy to enhance innovation and may not have immediate widespread effects.
reforms.source_quote: The government also announced the designation of regulation-free special zones.
